steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: In the function $f(x) = 2{,}400(0.80)^x$, the variable $x$ is years after 2011. Setting $x = 0$ gives the population at the starting year 2011: $f(0) = 2{,}400 \cdot (0.80)^0 = 2{,}400 \cdot 1 = 2{,}400$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: In any exponential model $f(x) = a \cdot b^x$, the coefficient $a$ is the initial value when $x = 0$. Here $a = 2{,}400$ is the population at $x = 0$, which corresponds to 2011.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Evaluate each choice:
  • The estimated percent decrease in the population for this species and preserve every $7$ years after $2011$
  • The estimated percent decrease in the population for this species and preserve each year after $2011$
  • The estimated population for this species and preserve $7$ years after $2011$
  • The matching choice: The estimated initial population for this species and preserve in $2011$ — D is correct.
